nodes.context: Italian-American Mafia family operating primarily in New York and New Jersey, involved in racketeering, gambling, loansharking, and labor corruption.

nodes.standard_name: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
nodes.original_text_name: New Jersey faction of the Genovese crime family
nodes.context: Sub-group of the Genovese family controlling organized crime in New Jersey, divided into crews.
New York Mafia family led by Joe Masseria, active during Prohibition and the Castellammarese War.
Brooklyn-based Mafia clan led by Salvatore Maranzano, rival of Masseria during the Castellammarese War.
Predecessor name for the Genovese crime family under Charles 'Lucky' Luciano.
New York Mafia family, later known as Lucchese family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
New York Mafia family, later known as Gambino family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
New York Mafia family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
Small Mafia family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
New York Mafia family, later known as Colombo family, involved in New Jersey territory division.
Mafia family based in Philadelphia, involved in New Jersey territory division.
New York Mafia family, rival of Genovese in Atlantic City dispute.
New York Mafia family, allied with Genovese in some periods.
Crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Tino Fiumara and Michael Coppola, controlling waterfront rackets.
Crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Angelo Prisco and Joseph LaScala, active in Hudson County waterfront.
A crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Louis Gatto and later his son Joseph Gatto, involved in gambling, loansharking, and bookmaking in Bergen and Passaic counties.
A crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Silvio DeVita, operating in Essex County with ties to Sicily.
A crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Ludwig Bruschi, involved in gambling, loansharking, and drug distribution in South and North Jersey.
New York Mafia family led by Joe Masseria, precursor to the Genovese family, involved in bootlegging during Prohibition.
One of the Five Families of New York, allied with the Genovese family.
New Jersey-based Mafia family, allied with the Genovese family.
Mafia family based in Detroit, Michigan, allied with the Genovese family.

nodes.standard_name: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction
nodes.context: New Jersey branch of the Lucchese crime family, allied with the Genovese family.
Philadelphia-based Irish-American gang, allied with the Genovese family.
A crew within the Genovese New Jersey faction, led by Tino Fiumara and later Stephen Depiro, controlling illegal operations at the Newark/Elizabeth Seaport.
A crew within the Genovese family, led by Jimmy Napoli, later taken over by Anthony Palumbo, involved in gambling and loansharking in Brooklyn.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.context: The New Jersey faction is a sub-group of the Genovese crime family.
The two families fought during the Castellammarese War for control of Italian gangs in the U.S.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.context: Early members of the New Jersey faction were under the control of Joe Masseria's family.

edges.source: Genovese crime family New Jersey faction
edges.context: After Masseria's murder, the New Jersey faction continued working for Luciano.
The Luciano family was the predecessor of the Genovese family.
The families disputed over Atlantic City territory and Manna plotted to kill John Gotti.
Genovese gained support from Lucchese boss Tommy Lucchese in the 1950s.

The Napoli crew was a crew within the Genovese family, later taken over by Anthony Palumbo.
The Masseria family and Castellammarese clan fought in the Castellammarese War for control of Italian gangs in the U.S.
The Luciano crime family succeeded the Masseria family after Masseria's murder.
The Genovese family is allied with the Bonanno family.
The Genovese family is allied with the Colombo family.
